About

London. Europe. Everywhere in between.

London-based executive coach and consultant with over two decades of international experience across Europe, Africa, and Central Asia. My practice sits at the intersection of leadership, culture, and change — working with senior leaders and organisations navigating complexity and transformation.

Grounded in Gestalt-based relational coaching. I hold the Advanced Practitioner Diploma in Executive Coaching from AoEC, Adaptive Leadership training, and draw on years of fieldwork in cultural strategy, creative production, and cross-cultural communication.

I work with CEOs, directors, cultural institutions, NGOs, and international foundations — helping people and organisations expand what is possible. Available globally, based in London.

The Journey

How I got here

I

International Foundations

Formed by work across cultures.

Two decades in international arts, culture, and social impact — across Europe, Sub-Saharan Africa, and Central Asia. From cultural institutes and NGOs to government advisory and creative production. A practice shaped by genuine cross-cultural fluency.

II

Gestalt Training · Relational Practice

A deeper turn toward the human.

The move into coaching came through Gestalt — a relational, body-informed, phenomenologically aware tradition. Not about fixing, but about expanding what is possible. This became the foundation for everything that followed.

III

AoEC 2026 · Adaptive Leadership Paris 2026

The current practice.

Completed the AoEC Advanced Practitioner Diploma in 2026 — fourteen months of rigorous coaching practice aligned to ICF PCC standards. Continuing professional development through Adaptive Leadership (Heifetz & Linsky framework) and ongoing supervision. Working with senior leaders internationally.

Credentials & Achievements

A practice grounded in continuous learning.

2026 · Latest Milestone New

Advanced Practitioner Diploma in Executive Coaching

AoEC — The Academy of Executive Coaching, London

Fourteen months of meaningful stretch — intellectually, emotionally, and personally. Reflective peer coaching, supervision, deep self-study, and the emergence of a personal coaching model.

Assessment included a 45-minute presentation on values, coaching influences, principles, and emerging model — alongside a live coaching demonstration assessed against ICF PCC standards.

“Leadership is about how we show up as real human beings for each other.”

WritingCommentary

Journal · Recent Reflections New

From the field.

April 2026 · Paris

Four days in Paris — on Adaptive Leadership, provocation, and sitting in the discomfort.

Immersive experience, deeply reflective, challenging, at times feeling confused. 24 Change Leaders from all over the world, joining under one roof. One idea kept surfacing: provocation as a tool and a way of leadership…

Immersive experience, deeply reflective, challenging, at times feeling confused… Expanding and growing new muscles on capacity to grasp the idea of Adaptive Leadership and learning about interventions.

24 Change Leaders from all over the world joining together under one roof from early morning until late evening — bringing diversity, inclusivity, global expansion and cross-cultural perspectives, their values, knowledge and great experiences, real cases to work on.

One idea that kept surfacing throughout the workshop was provocation as a tool and a way of leadership. It is a delicate dance of purposeful provocation that helps a group surface what is uncomfortable, unspoken, or avoided. The kind that invites people to confront the realities needed for meaningful change.

Adaptive leadership isn’t about providing answers. It’s about creating the conditions where people can grapple with the hard questions together.

A few reflections & takeaways from the four days:

  1. Leadership lives in the tension.
  2. Real change requires sitting in the discomfort between what is familiar and what is necessary.
  3. Provocation can be a catalyst. Thoughtful challenge can open up conversations that teams often avoid — surfacing assumptions, patterns, and hidden dynamics.
  4. Get on the balcony. One of the most powerful leadership acts is stepping back to observe the system: noticing patterns, behaviours, alliances, and what is not being said.
  5. Distinguish technical problems from adaptive challenges. Technical issues have clear solutions. Adaptive challenges require shifts in mindset, behaviour, and shared ownership.
  6. The work is deeply human. Change is rarely about strategy alone. It involves identity, loss, fear, power, and values.

My biggest reflection: leadership is less about control and more about holding space for learning, tension, and progress. Also feeling confused & messy too…

Grateful for the challenge, the continued stretch in my own expansion, the deep reflections, the conversations & the provocation.

#AdaptiveLeadership   #Reflections   #MessyLeadership   #Paris2026

2026

Completing the AoEC Advanced Diploma

Fourteen months of reflective practice, peer coaching, supervision. The most valuable learning wasn’t new techniques — it was how we show up as real human beings for each other.

2026

Côte d’Azur — Notes Between Sessions

On the importance of spaciousness in a full calendar, and working in beautiful places as a kind of teaching in itself.

2026

On Continuing Development as Identity Work

Why senior practitioners still need to keep learning — and why the deepest development is rarely about adding more techniques.

Services

What I do.

01

Coaching · Practice

Executive Coaching

Deep one-to-one coaching for senior leaders and executives. Six to twelve month engagements, ICF PCC-aligned, grounded in Gestalt relational practice. For leaders expanding into greater complexity, seeking clarity, or navigating significant professional transition.

Senior LeadersICF PCCGestalt

02

Leadership · Programmes

Leadership Development

Bespoke leadership programmes for organisations investing in their leadership culture. Combines individual coaching, facilitated workshops, systemic enquiry, and 360 feedback into integrated programmes that shift culture, not just capability.

ProgrammesOrganisational

03

Strategy · Advisory

Cultural Strategy & Consulting

Strategic advisory for cultural organisations, international foundations, NGOs, and government ministries. Cultural identity, programme architecture, social impact design, creative production, and international partnership development.

Cultural StrategyAdvisory

04

Cross-Cultural · International

Cross-Cultural Leadership Coaching

Coaching for leaders operating across cultures, languages, and geographies. Building intercultural intelligence, adaptive communication, and culturally-sensitive leadership presence — drawing on fieldwork across Europe, Africa, and Central Asia.

InternationalIntercultural

05

Teams · Change

Team & Change Coaching

Working with leadership teams through periods of transition, restructure, or strategic renewal. Systemic team coaching that improves relational dynamics, collective decision-making, and shared purpose — informed by Adaptive Leadership principles.

TeamsAdaptive

Frequently Asked

Before we begin.

Senior executives, directors, and high-potential leaders across sectors — from CEOs of international organisations to directors of cultural institutions, NGO leaders, and executives in professional services. My sweet spot is leaders navigating complexity, transition, or cross-cultural contexts.
Gestalt coaching is a relational, body-informed, and phenomenologically aware approach. In practice this means spacious sessions that engage the whole person — thoughts, feelings, bodily awareness, and relational patterns. It is not about fixing what is broken; it is about expanding what is possible.
Most executive coaching engagements run six to twelve months, with sessions fortnightly. This gives the relationship time to deepen and allows for real change to integrate. Shorter focused programmes are also possible for specific transitions or challenges.
Both. Dana works internationally — meeting clients in person in London and across Europe, and remotely with clients globally. Many engagements blend both modes. All conversations are confidential.
Consulting engagements are scoped individually — from strategic advisory relationships to hands-on programme design and delivery. I work with cultural organisations, foundations, NGOs, and government ministries. Send a brief outline of your context and what you’re exploring, and we’ll take it from there.
With a complimentary 45-minute discovery conversation. No obligation. It’s an honest exploration of what you’re seeking and whether we are well-matched. Book via the contact form below or email directly.
